reasons: Eudora can burst a target Franco has hooked or suppressed.
Franco's single-target control gives Eudora a stable window to land her magic combo.

proof.summary: Franco isolates one target with hook or suppression, and Eudora follows with single-target magic burst during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a squishy mage or marksman.
Eudora is in range with full combo ready.
Both heroes focus the same target after the catch.
failureCases: Franco hooks a tank or bait target.
Eudora is out of range when the hook lands.
Enemy shield, magic defense, or peel keeps the target alive.

reasons: Saber can follow Franco's hook or suppression with Triple Sweep burst.
Franco's catch creates the isolated target that Saber wants for a single-target pickoff.

proof.summary: Franco catches a priority target, and Saber chains airborne burst onto the same isolated enemy.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a fragile backline target.
Saber is close enough to follow immediately.
The enemy team is not already positioned to peel.
failureCases: Franco misses or hooks a durable target.
Saber dives before the catch is confirmed.
Enemy peel interrupts Saber after the control window.

reasons: Karina can enter after Franco isolates a target with hook or suppression.
Franco's pickoff window helps Karina finish a target and look for reset pressure.

proof.summary: Franco creates the controlled target, while Karina follows with burst mobility to finish the pick.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a low-HP or fragile enemy.
Karina waits for the hook or suppression before entering.
Karina has cooldowns available to continue after the kill.
failureCases: Franco pulls a tank into the team.
Karina enters into grouped enemy peel.
The target survives and Karina loses reset pressure.

reasons: Kadita can follow Franco's hook or suppression with delayed knock-up and burst.
Franco's control window helps Kadita time Breath of the Ocean and Rough Waves on the caught target.

proof.summary: Franco isolates or suppresses a target, while Kadita uses the control window to connect her knock-up timing and burst combo.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a squishy or priority target.
Kadita is close enough to follow immediately.
Kadita times Breath of the Ocean during Franco's control window.
failureCases: Franco hooks a tank or bait target.
Kadita is too far and the control window expires.
Enemy peel, cleanse, or saved mobility breaks the follow-up timing.

reasons: Vexana can land magic burst on a target Franco has hooked or suppressed.
Franco's control gives Vexana time to place her follow-up damage and add zoning pressure.

proof.summary: Franco holds a target in place, and Vexana follows with magic burst plus zoning pressure around the caught enemy.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a squishy target within Vexana's range.
Vexana has key cooldowns ready when the catch lands.
The target is near teammates for extra AoE or zoning value.
failureCases: Vexana is too far to follow the hook.
The target is too durable for the burst window.
Enemy peel prevents Vexana from finishing the target.
